groups.label: Management and Informatics
groups.benchFacingDecisionCue: Protect patient reporting, choose the oversight or workflow path, and keep operational consequences visible.

groups.label: Foundations
groups.benchFacingDecisionCue: Start from specimen or patient context, select the safest bench check, and route the result without skipping safeguards.

groups.label: Transfusion
groups.benchFacingDecisionCue: Stabilize transfusion safety first, reconcile identity and compatibility, then decide escalation or safe unit selection.

groups.label: Microbiology
groups.benchFacingDecisionCue: Anchor organism or specimen quality to source context before reporting identification, susceptibility, or recollection decisions.

routes.title: Anemia Workup and Hemolysis Triage
routes.benchFacingDecisionCue: Separate increased red-cell loss from impaired production and from patterns that need urgent marrow-level escalation.
routes.summary: Use CBC indices, reticulocyte response, and hemolysis cues to decide whether anemia reflects blood loss or hemolysis, reduced production, or urgent marrow pathology.

routes.title: Bleeding Diathesis and Coagulation Test Interpretation
routes.benchFacingDecisionCue: Distinguish platelet or von Willebrand-type bleeding from factor pathway problems and unstable systemic coagulopathy.
routes.summary: Use bleeding pattern, platelet data, and coagulation screening tests to decide whether the problem is primary hemostasis, factor pathway dysfunction, or unstable systemic coagulopathy.

routes.title: Specimen-to-Result CP Workflow
routes.benchFacingDecisionCue: A flawed specimen should redirect the workflow before deeper bench reasoning begins.
routes.summary: Move a Clinical Pathology request from specimen quality triage and specimen integrity through bench selection, result interpretation, and safe reporting without skipping preanalytic or postanalytic safeguards.

routes.title: Finance-Aware Assay Planning
routes.benchFacingDecisionCue: Pick the operational bottleneck before committing to in-house rollout or a platform contract.
routes.summary: Plan an assay rollout by balancing break-even logic, acquisition structure, and operational burden before bringing testing in-house.

routes.title: LIS Workflow Redesign
routes.benchFacingDecisionCue: Choose the intervention by matching it to the dominant risk rather than defaulting to a favorite informatics tool.
routes.summary: Redesign an unsafe reporting workflow by deciding whether interfacing, middleware, or process redesign is the highest-yield safety intervention.

routes.title: QC Failure Response
routes.benchFacingDecisionCue: Separate isolated random-error patterns from systematic failure and post-change instability.
routes.summary: Operational triage for a QC failure: characterize the control pattern, protect patient reporting, and choose the next corrective action.

routes.title: Validation-Versus-Verification Triage
routes.benchFacingDecisionCue: Separate truly new or materially altered claims from local confirmation of expected performance.
routes.summary: Choose the right oversight path when a lab is bringing on a test, modifying an assay, or restarting a changed method.

routes.title: AST Interpretation
routes.benchFacingDecisionCue: Susceptibility categories are only meaningful when the isolate itself is clinically relevant and the specimen source supports treatment-level interpretation.
routes.summary: Interpret antimicrobial susceptibility results only after organism identity, source context, and reporting consequences are aligned, then decide when to suppress, escalate, or communicate a high-risk result.

routes.title: Clinical Microbiology Specimen Quality Triage
routes.benchFacingDecisionCue: Use specimen source, contamination risk, transport delay, and adequacy to decide whether the sample should be worked up, cautiously limited, or rejected.
routes.summary: Decide whether a microbiology specimen is acceptable for culture or molecular workup, when recollection is safer, and when a limited bench path can still yield a trustworthy result.

routes.title: Organism Identification Workflow
routes.benchFacingDecisionCue: Use Gram stain, colony pattern, growth conditions, or rapid platform fit to determine the next identification branch instead of reflexively applying every method.
routes.summary: Choose the next safest organism-identification path by combining Gram stain, growth pattern, source context, and bench method selection before attaching clinical meaning or susceptibility consequences.

routes.title: Crossmatch Workup
routes.benchFacingDecisionCue: Decide whether this is a routine compatibility issue or an acute transfusion-safety event requiring immediate reaction workflow.
routes.summary: Stepwise blood-bank algorithm for crossmatch incompatibility, specimen identification checks, antibody-screen correlation, and safe unit-selection escalation.

routes.title: Transfusion Reaction Triage
routes.benchFacingDecisionCue: Treat fever, chills, dyspnea, hypotension, flank pain, dark urine, urticaria, or rapid cardiopulmonary change during or after transfusion as a true reaction pathway until proven otherwise.
routes.summary: Use immediate symptom pattern, transfusion-safety steps, and blood-bank reasoning to separate hemolytic, febrile or allergic, and pulmonary or volume-overload transfusion reactions.
